device_name: APAS Independence with Urine Analysis Module
intended_use: The APAS Independence is an in vitro diagnostic system comprised of an instrument and software analysis module(s) for specific indications that are used to automate imaging and interpretation of micro
intended_use_full: The APAS Independence is an in vitro diagnostic system comprised of an instrument and software analysis module(s) for specific indications that are used to automate imaging and interpretation of microbial colonies on plates of solid culture media.
ground_truth: Comparison between APAS Compact (predicate device) and APAS Independence using paired plate images from clinical urine samples
other_metrics: Blood agar plate designation agreement: Positive 98.7% (95% CI: 96.3-99.6%), Review 86.5% (95% CI: 72.0-94.1%), Negative 79.5% (95% CI: 69.2-87.0%). MacConkey agar plate designation agreement: Positive 99.3% (95% CI: 95.9-99.9%), Review 80.0% (95% CI: 37.6-96.4%), Negative 89.1% (95% CI: 84.2-92.6%). Colony morphology detection on blood agar: Alpha hemolysis 97.1% (95% CI: 94.8-98.4%), Beta hemolysis 96.3% (95% CI: 93.7-97.8%), Coliform 98.9% (95% CI: 97.1-99.6%), Cream white 95.1% (95% CI: 92.4-96.9%), Granular 99.7% (95% CI: 98.4-99.9%), Small 98.3% (95% CI: 96.3-99.2%), Swarming 100% (95% CI: 98.9-100%). Colony morphology detection on MacConkey agar: Lactose fermenter 99.1% (95% CI: 97.5-99.7%), Non-fermenter 99.4% (95% CI: 97.9-99.8%), Non-pigmented 100% (95% CI: 98.9-100%), Red Pink 100% (95% CI: 98.9-100%).
